claim: Context window size is the fundamental constraint limiting multi-agent systems - you must filter tables/data relevant to queries rather than passing everything
actionable: In multi-agent database systems, implement table/data filtering before passing to LLMs; use vector embeddings + string matching to select only relevant tables
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: String matching for table selection outperforms vector embeddings by 100x in practical database applications - use embeddings as fallback, not primary
actionable: For table/field selection in databases, first check if the table name appears literally in the natural language query before relying on vector similarity search
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: Token counting and cost estimation must be built into orchestrator systems to understand real spend and identify which agents are too expensive
actionable: Add cost/token tracking to every agent workflow; use this data to identify high-cost agents and remove them if ROI doesn't justify the spend
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: GPT-4 costs 3 cents per 1000 input tokens and 6 cents per 1000 output tokens - data visualization agents often consume more tokens than they produce value
actionable: When optimizing agent costs, consider removing formatting/visualization agents and writing results to files instead - they consume expensive model invocations without proportional value
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: Returning large SQL result sets (thousands of rows) directly to LLM agents creates token explosion - instead write results to files for post-processing
actionable: For database queries returning many rows, have agents write results to files and report file paths back, don't pass row data through LLM context
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: Building commodity AI applications now requires focus on specific problems with strong business logic rather than generic solutions - specificity creates defensibility
actionable: When building on top of LLMs, solve specific vertical problems with custom business logic, not horizontal AI wrapper products
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: Open source models (Llama, Mistral) are not competitive with GPT-4 for complex agentic tasks - the capability gap is too large to bridge with fine-tuning
actionable: For complex multi-agent systems and data analytics, use GPT-4 rather than open source models; the capability difference justifies the cost
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: Parallel agents analyzing the same data with different expertise is valuable for synthesizing recommendations, but requires good orchestration
actionable: Structure multi-agent systems with independent specialist agents run in parallel, then use a synthesizer agent to merge findings and rank by priority
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: The 'GPT wrapper' criticism is overblown - good SaaS is just as much a wrapper on underlying technology, value comes from UX and business logic
actionable: Don't worry about being called a GPT wrapper; focus on specific problem-solving and good UX; all software is essentially wrapping underlying infrastructure
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks

claim: Agentic systems that work with LLMs in parallel to users can deliver exponential value gains - this represents a new category of software
actionable: When building AI systems, optimize for parallel agentic execution that augments human capability simultaneously rather than sequential workflows
source_title: AutoGen Token Tactics: FIRING AI Agents, USELESS Vector Embeddings, GPT-4 Memory Tricks
